entries.title: 2.1L Diesel
entries.history: Top UK GLK diesel known for fleet efficiency but plagued by early timing chain tensioner failures.

commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear and rattling (typically 60,000-90,000 miles)
Turbo actuator sticking due to carbon buildup
High-pressure fuel pump sealing issues

commonProblems.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mercedes-Benz GLK Class X204 generation (2008-2015) is generally robust, but the OM651 four-cylinder diesel, OM642 V6 diesel, and M272 V6 petrol engines each have well-documented weak points that drive replacement enquiries. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components specifically designed to address these original failure points, backed by a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Use the breakdowns below as a decision tool to compare repair versus replacement costs before committing to a workshop.

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class OM651 Timing Chain Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class OM651 Timing Chain Failure - Why Early Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 200 CDI, 220 CDI, 250 CDI (2008-2011, OM651.901, OM651.911, OM651.921)
problems.rootCause: Early OM651 units feature a rear-mounted timing chain with a hydraulic tensioner that loses pressure prematurely. This causes chain stretch, guide wear, and a distinctive rattling noise on cold starts. If ignored, the chain can snap, causing catastrophic valve and piston collision.

repairOptions.tier: Minor - Preventative Timing Chain Kit Replacement
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Full tensioner upgrade, chain guides, and rail replacement without engine removal
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only if caught early before chain stretch or valve contact occurs

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned OM651 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with upgraded timing chain kit, new injectors, and EGR components fitted as standard
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent solution with 12-month warranty; eliminates all pre-facelift weak points

problems.recommendation: If your Mercedes-Benz GLK Class OM651 is exhibiting a cold-start rattle below 70,000 miles, the preventative chain kit replacement is cost-effective. However, if the engine has already suffered timing jump or oil pressure loss, a minor repair is a financial risk. We recommend a fully rebuilt OM651 unit, which includes the upgraded tensioner as standard and restores long-term reliability.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM651 Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine prices for your variant

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class OM642 Oil Cooler Seal & EGR Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class OM642 Oil Cooler Seal & EGR Failure - The V6 Diesel Weak Point
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 320 CDI, 350 CDI (2008-2015, OM642.920, OM642.930)
problems.rootCause: The OM642 V6 places the oil cooler and EGR cooler in a tight valley between the cylinder banks. Factory rubber seals degrade under sustained heat, allowing oil and coolant to mix. Prolonged neglect leads to hydrostatic lock, bearing washout, and terminal engine seizure.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Oil Cooler & EGR Seal Overhaul
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Engine valley access, cooler removal, upgraded silicone seal installation, and coolant flush
repairOptions.longevity: Only addresses the leak; does not reverse existing bearing wear or coolant contamination

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned OM642 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete V6 swap with upgraded oil cooler seals, new EGR cooler, and bench-tested bottom end
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory performance with upgraded sealing materials and full 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: The OM642 oil leak is progressive, not sudden. If caught early during routine servicing, the specialist seal overhaul extends engine life affordably. Once coolant has entered the oil pan or metallic debris is present, patch repairs are futile. For high-mileage Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 350 CDI models, a rebuilt OM642 delivers superior long-term value and peace of mind.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt OM642 Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine quotes now

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class M272 Balance Shaft Failure
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class M272 Balance Shaft Failure - Petrol V6 Knocking & Timing Issues
problems.rootCause: Early M272 V6 engines use a soft-metal balance shaft gear that wears prematurely due to insufficient surface hardening. This creates a metallic knocking sound, disrupts valve timing, and eventually causes the timing chain to skip teeth. The resulting interference bends valves and destroys the cylinder head.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Top-end access, new chains, hydraulic tensioners, and guide rails replaced
repairOptions.longevity: Does not address the underlying balance shaft wear; failure will recur within 20,000 miles

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned M272 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete V6 replacement featuring factory-corrected balance shaft gear, updated timing kit, and full calibration
repairOptions.longevity: Permanent resolution with verified hard-shaft components and 12-month unlimited mileage cover

problems.recommendation: Do not attempt a timing-only repair on a Mercedes-Benz GLK Class with confirmed M272 balance shaft wear. The gear degradation is internal and irreversible. We advise fitting a fully rebuilt M272 unit, which includes the corrected hard-faced balance shaft as standard, saving thousands versus dealer replacement and eliminating repeat failure risk.
problems.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt M272 Mercedes-Benz GLK Class V6 engines

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class M271 Supercharger & Timing Chain Rattle
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class M271 Supercharger & Timing Chain Rattle - 1.8L Petrol Degradation
problems.rootCause: The M271 supercharged engine combines high cylinder pressures with a known weak timing tensioner. As the tensioner fails, the chain stretches, while the supercharger coupling bearings simultaneously wear out. Combined boost loss and timing drift quickly lead to poor compression and piston-to-valve contact.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Timing tensioner replacement, supercharger clutch inspection, and chain tension reset
repairOptions.longevity: Effective only if performed at first rattle sign; prevents secondary cylinder head damage

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned M271 Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete engine swap with upgraded tensioner, new supercharger bearings, and recalibrated ECU mapping
repairOptions.longevity: Restores factory boost and timing stability, backed by specialist warranty and UK delivery

problems.recommendation: The M271 supercharger and timing failure are deeply linked. If your Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 200 exhibits boost lag alongside timing rattle, separate repairs will only postpone inevitable failure. A rebuilt M271 unit resolves both systems simultaneously, offering better long-term economics than piecemeal dealer interventions.
problems.cta: Compare M271 Mercedes-Benz GLK Class replacement engine prices today

problems.group: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class Turbocharger & EGR Cooler Blockage
problems.h4: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class Turbocharger & EGR Cooler Blockage - Diesel Power Loss & Warning Lights
problems.affectedModels: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 220 CDI, 250 CDI, 320 CDI (2008-2015, OM651 & OM642 families)
problems.rootCause: Short urban trips and delayed servicing cause carbon accumulation in the EGR cooler and turbo vanes. Restricted exhaust flow increases backpressure, overheating the turbo bearings and forcing the engine into limp mode. Ignoring limp-mode warnings accelerates bearing seizure and oil starvation.

repairOptions.tier: Intermediate - Turbo Clean, EGR Replacement & Decarbonising
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Turbo vane actuator calibration, EGR cooler swap, walnut blasting of intake ports, and DPF regeneration
repairOptions.longevity: Restores mid-range torque if bearings are intact; does not fix seized turbo or oil contamination

repairOptions.tier: Full Replacement - Rebuilt / Reconditioned Diesel Engine
repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Complete CDI unit swap with new turbocharger, upgraded EGR, and full ancillary belt refresh
repairOptions.longevity: Comprehensive reset of the emissions and forced induction system with 12-month warranty

problems.recommendation: If your Mercedes-Benz GLK Class is pulling over in limp mode with turbo warning lights, a specialist diagnostic should precede any purchase. Where the turbo core remains serviceable, the intermediate EGR and decarbonising service restores performance cost-effectively. For engines showing metallic turbo debris or oil in the intercooler, a rebuilt CDI engine prevents catastrophic secondary damage and ensures MoT compliance.
problems.cta: Compare specialist Mercedes-Benz GLK Class diesel engine replacement quotes

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mercedes-Benz GLK Class
finalCta.paragraph: Whether you are facing an OM651 timing chain rattle, OM642 oil leak, or M272 balance shaft knock, EngineMarket removes the guesswork and dealer premium from your replacement decision. Our UK network connects you directly with vetted Mercedes-Benz GLK Class specialists who supply and fit rebuilt engines featuring upgraded failure-point components, bench testing, and a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty. Compare transparent prices, confirm exact variant compatibility, and get your X204 back on the road with nationwide delivery and specialist backing.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLK Class Engine Replacement Prices Now →

engineSizes.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class Engines by Size - Find the Right Displacement for Your Variant
engineSizes.intro: Every engine displacement officially fitted to the Mercedes-Benz GLK Class is listed below, complete with compatible factory codes, production years, and documented failure notes to ensure accurate part matching.

groups.title: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class Diesel Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 2.1L Diesel Engines
items.description: Serving as the backbone of the X204 generation’s diesel lineup, this compact four-cylinder unit powered the vast majority of UK models from launch through to 2015. The engine family introduced advanced common-rail injection and, in later facelift versions, integrated AdBlue technology to meet stricter Euro 5 emissions standards without sacrificing low-end torque.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 2.1L diesel engines

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Positioned as the premium towing and motorway option, this smooth V6 diesel delivered refined power and superior high-speed stability across both pre-facelift and updated X204 chassis. Engineers designed the unit with a robust bottom end and sophisticated variable valve timing, though its tightly packaged valley area required precise thermal management.
items.cta: Get quotes for Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 3.0L diesel engines

groups.title: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class Petrol Engine Sizes

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 1.8L Petrol Engines
items.description: Offering the entry-level petrol experience for the GLK Class, this supercharged inline-four paired brisk acceleration with manageable running costs during the model’s early years. The unit relied on forced induction to compensate for its smaller displacement, delivering a characteristically linear power delivery that appealed to urban drivers before the 2011 facelift shifted focus to larger V6 options.
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 1.8L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Designed to bridge the gap between efficiency and refinement, this naturally aspirated V6 provided a smoother alternative to the supercharged four-cylinder while maintaining respectable fuel economy for its class. The architecture featured an aluminium block and dual overhead camshafts, delivering quiet, vibration-free operation that aligned well with the SUV’s premium interior positioning.
commonFailurePoints: Balance shaft gear wear causing metallic knocking
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 3.5L Petrol Engines
items.description: Acting as the performance flagship for the petrol range, this high-output V6 evolved across two generations to address early reliability concerns while boosting overall output and emissions compliance. The updated direct-injection variant introduced for the facelift significantly improved throttle response and reduced carbon-related issues, cementing its reputation as the most refined combustion option in the lineup.
commonFailurePoints: direct injection intake valve carbon buildup
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLK Class 3.5L petrol engine prices

engineYears.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This technical timeline covers all major engine changes across the Mercedes-Benz GLK Class X204 production run (2008-2015), organised so owners can quickly identify their engine code and compare rebuilt engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: X204 Launch Year: OM651 & M272 Debut
years.description: The GLK Class launched with the new OM651 four-cylinder diesel and M272 V6 petrol families, establishing the engine lineup that would define the entire X204 generation.
keyChanges: X204 platform introduced as Mercedes-Benz compact SUV entry point
OM651.901/911 diesel engines replaced older M111/M646 units
M271 supercharged petrol and M272 V6 petrol options launched alongside diesel range
4MATIC all-wheel drive available across most variants from day one
knownFor: OM651 early-model timing chain tensioner vulnerability emerges in UK fleet usage
M272 V6 balance shaft gear wear risk on pre-2009 production blocks
Strong motorway refinement and towing capability from OM642 V6 diesel
High initial demand for GLK 220 CDI as company car and fleet choice

keyChanges: GLK 250 CDI introduced with twin-turbo OM651.921 delivering 204hp
GLK 350 CDI launched with updated OM642.920 V6 producing 265hp
GLK 300 petrol variant added using revised M272.948 tuning
4MATIC availability extended to new diesel performance variants
knownFor: OM651.921 twin-turbo setup delivers strong mid-range torque but adds turbo actuator complexity
OM642.920 V6 oil cooler seal leaks begin appearing on higher-mileage UK examples
M272 balance shaft wear remains a concern for pre-facelift petrol V6 owners
GLK 350 CDI becomes the premium towing choice for UK SUV buyers

Minor ECU updates to improve emissions compliance and fuel economy
Increased UK fleet penetration driving higher mileage OM651 replacement demand

OM651.941 BlueTEC engines with AdBlue dosing introduced for emissions compliance

faq.h2: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine replacement cost UK?
items.answer: A Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine replacement typically costs £2,156 to £6,847 for a rebuilt unit supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Diesel OM651 units start around £2,200, while V6 OM642 or M276 petrol engines reach £5,000+. Supply and fit adds £700-£1,300. Prices vary by damage severity, mileage, and specialist location within the UK network.
keyPoints: OM651 2.1L diesel rebuilt engines average £2,200-£3,400 supply-only UK
OM642 3.0L V6 diesel replacements range £3,500-£5,300 depending on variant
M272/M276 petrol V6 engines cost £3,400-£5,900 for rebuilt units
Supply and fit installation typically adds £700-£1,300 to engine price
All rebuilt engines include minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine replacement prices from UK specialists now

items.question: Is the OM651 timing chain problem really that bad in the Mercedes-Benz GLK Class?
items.answer: Yes, early Mercedes-Benz GLK Class OM651 engines (2008-2011) suffer rear-mounted timing chain tensioner failures between 60,000-90,000 miles. The rattling noise on cold starts indicates imminent chain stretch. Ignoring it risks valve-piston collision and catastrophic engine failure requiring full replacement.
keyPoints: OM651 timing chain tensioner wear affects GLK 200/220/250 CDI pre-facelift models
Cold-start rattling is the primary warning sign before catastrophic failure occurs
Rebuilt OM651 engines include upgraded tensioner kits as standard prevention measure
Facelift OM651 units (2012+) feature revised tensioner design reducing failure risk
items.cta: Get quotes for rebuilt OM651 Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engines with timing kit included

items.question: Which Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine is most reliable - diesel or petrol?
items.answer: Facelift Mercedes-Benz GLK Class diesel OM651 engines (2012-2015) offer the best reliability balance, with revised timing components and strong longevity when serviced properly. Petrol M276 V6 units are refined but costlier to repair. Early OM651 and M272 engines carry higher failure risks requiring preventative attention.
keyPoints: Post-2012 OM651 diesel engines feature upgraded timing tensioners reducing failure rates significantly
M276 direct-injection petrol V6 resolves M272 balance shaft issues but needs carbon cleaning
OM642 V6 diesel offers robust towing capability but requires oil cooler seal monitoring
Regular oil changes every 10,000 miles extend all GLK engine lifespans considerably
items.cta: Compare reliable Mercedes-Benz GLK Class diesel engine options from UK specialists

items.question: What causes the OM642 V6 oil leak in the Mercedes-Benz GLK 350 CDI?
items.answer: Mercedes-Benz GLK Class OM642 V6 oil leaks stem from degraded oil cooler and EGR cooler seals located in the engine valley. Heat cycling causes rubber seals to harden and crack, allowing oil and coolant to mix. Left unchecked, contamination leads to bearing washout and terminal engine seizure.
keyPoints: OM642 oil cooler seals fail between 80,000-130,000 miles on GLK 350 CDI models
Coolant mixing with oil creates milky sludge visible on dipstick or filler cap
Rebuilt OM642 engines include upgraded silicone seals preventing repeat leakage issues
Early detection via regular oil checks can avoid catastrophic bottom-end damage
items.cta: Get quotes for OM642 Mercedes-Benz GLK Class rebuilt engines with seal upgrades

items.question: Is it worth replacing the engine in a Mercedes-Benz GLK Class or should I sell?
items.answer: Replacing a Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine is usually worthwhile if the chassis, transmission, and interior remain in good condition. A rebuilt engine costing £2,200-£5,300 extends vehicle life significantly versus part-exchange values of £3,000-£8,000. Factor in warranty coverage and specialist fitting to maximise return on investment.
keyPoints: GLK Class X204 models retain strong residual values when mechanically sound and well-maintained
Rebuilt engines include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty protecting your investment long-term
Supply and fit by CDI-trained specialists ensures correct installation and preserves resale value
Compare total replacement cost against part-exchange offers before deciding to sell
items.cta: Compare Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine replacement costs versus selling your vehicle

items.question: How long do Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engines last with proper maintenance?
items.answer: Well-maintained Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engines routinely reach 150,000-200,000 miles before major work is needed. OM651 diesel units with timely timing chain service and OM642 V6 engines with seal monitoring demonstrate exceptional longevity. Petrol M276 facelift engines also prove durable when direct-injection carbon buildup is managed properly.
keyPoints: Regular oil changes every 10,000 miles prevent premature wear across all GLK engine families
OM651 timing chain inspection at 60,000 miles avoids catastrophic failure on early models
OM642 oil cooler seal checks during servicing prevent coolant contamination and bearing damage
Using Mercedes-approved spec oils maintains turbocharger and injection system longevity
items.cta: Find long-life rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engines with warranty coverage UK

items.question: What's the difference between used, rebuilt and remanufactured Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engines?
items.answer: Used Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engines are removed from donor vehicles with variable mileage and limited testing. Rebuilt units replace worn internal components with upgraded parts addressing known failures like OM651 timing chains. Remanufactured engines receive full factory-spec restoration with new components throughout, offering maximum reliability at premium pricing.
keyPoints: Used engines cost £1,100-£2,400 but carry unknown history and limited warranty coverage
Rebuilt engines (£2,200-£5,300) include failure-point upgrades and 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
Remanufactured units (£3,600-£5,800) use new OEM components throughout for near-factory reliability
All options supplied through EngineMarket include bench testing and specialist verification
items.cta: Compare used, rebuilt and remanufactured Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine prices now

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Mercedes-Benz GLK Class?
items.answer: Fitting a different engine code in a Mercedes-Benz GLK Class requires extensive ECU recalibration, wiring modifications, and emissions compliance verification. While OM651 variants share architecture, mixing pre-facelift and facelift codes or swapping diesel for petrol creates complex compatibility challenges. Specialist assessment is essential before attempting any engine code interchange.
keyPoints: OM651.901, .911, .921 and .941 share block design but differ in turbo and emissions systems
ECU coding and immobiliser matching required for any engine code swap on GLK Class
Emissions compliance (Euro 5/6) must be maintained to pass MoT after engine replacement
Specialist fitting networks handle coding and calibration ensuring legal roadworthy installation
items.cta: Speak to Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine specialists about compatible replacement options

items.question: Does a rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine come with a warranty?
items.answer: Yes, every rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine supplied through EngineMarket includes a minimum 12-month unlimited mileage warranty from UK specialists. Coverage protects against manufacturing defects and component failure on upgraded parts like OM651 timing kits and OM642 seal upgrades. Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before purchase.
keyPoints: 12-month unlimited mileage warranty covers rebuilt OM651, OM642 and M272/M276 engine replacements
Warranty applies to supply-only and supply-and-fit installations across UK specialist network
Upgraded failure-point components (timing chains, seals) included under same warranty terms
Bench testing documentation provided with every rebuilt GLK Class engine for peace of mind
items.cta: Get quotes for warranted rebuilt Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engines from UK specialists

items.answer: Enter your registration number above to instantly identify your Mercedes-Benz GLK Class engine code via DVLA database lookup. Alternatively, check the VIN plate under the bonnet or service book for codes like OM651.911, OM642.920, or M276.957. Accurate code verification ensures compatible replacement engine selection and avoids costly fitting errors.

Engine code stamped on block near oil filter housing visible during inspection or servicing
OM651 variants power GLK 200/220/250 CDI while OM642 serves GLK 320/350 CDI models
M272/M276 codes identify petrol V6 engines in GLK 280/300/350 variants respectively
